messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Using LLMs for R coding and package development - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using LLMs for R coding and package development - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is thrilled to offer a 3-day seminar, Using LLMs for R Coding and Package Development <https://instats.org/seminar/using-llms-for-r-coding-and-package-deve>, running May 20 - 22 by professors Seth Flaxman from the University of Oxford and Leonid Chindelevitch from Imperial College London. This seminar is designed to equip researchers and PhD students with the skills necessary to efficiently integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) into their research workflows using R. By mastering tools such as ChatGPT and Claude, participants will gain a competitive edge in R coding and package development. The seminar allows participants to explore practical applications, engage in real-world coding scenarios, and learn techniques for optimizing R code. Harness the synergy of traditional coding and AI-powered tools to transform your research methodologies, while enhancing your coding efficiency and staying at the forefront of technological advances in data science and quantitative research.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-llms-for-r-coding-and-package-deve> to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>

Follow Instats:?Bluesky <https://bsky.app/profile/instats.bsky.social>_Linkedin <https://linkedin.com/company/instats1>_Facebook <https://www.facebook.com/InstatsTraining/>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Master Movement Ecology with R – Live Online Course | 12–16 May 2025
messages.subject_clean: Master Movement Ecology with R – Live Online Course | 12–16 May 2025
messages.body_plain:
*Master Movement Ecology with R ? Live Online Course | 12?16 May 2025*

We?re pleased to announce our upcoming live online course:
Movement Ecology Using R (MOVE07)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/movement-ecology-using-rmove07/>

12th?16th May 2025

Delivered by Prof. Luca Borga and Prof. Garrett Street

This course is ideal for researchers, postgraduate students, and ecologists
seeking a practical, structured approach to analysing movement data?from
GPS tracking to capture-mark-recapture?through the lens of a unified
Movement Ecology framework.

*Key features:*

   -

   Fully live format with two instructors always available during lectures
   and practicals
   -

   All sessions are recorded and accessible for three months after the
   course
   -

   Designed for applications across a wide range of species, from
   microorganisms to vertebrates
   -

   Strong emphasis on the ?question-driven? approach to study design,
   analysis, and application
   -

   Practical, hands-on sessions using R and real ecological datasets
   -

   Integration of theory with application, including management and
   communication of results

This course provides a comprehensive overview of the tools, technologies,
and models used in movement ecology and equips attendees with the knowledge
to apply them effectively in their own research.

*Course details and registration:*

https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-movement-ecology-move07/

Please feel free to share this with colleagues or students who may benefit.

If you have any questions, contact Oliver Hooker at:

*oliverhooker at prstatistics.com <oliverhooker at prstatistics.com>*


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Advance Your Data Science Skills with Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
messages.subject_clean: Advance Your Data Science Skills with Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
messages.body_plain:
*Advance Your Data Science Skills with Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal
Modelling using R-INLA*

Are you looking to take your spatial data analysis skills to the next level?

Join our Spatial and Spatial-Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-and-spatial-temporal-modelling-using-r-inla-sstm02/>course,
designed for researchers and data professionals working with spatial and
spatiotemporal data in ecology, epidemiology, environmental science, and
beyond.

*Course dates:* 23?27 June 2025

Led by *Prof. Virgilio G?mez Rubio*, a leading expert in Bayesian spatial
modelling and author of *Bayesian Inference with INLA
<https://www.routledge.com/Bayesian-inference-with-INLA/Gomez-Rubio/p/book/9781138039872>*,
this 5-day course will give you hands-on experience with:

   -

   Building spatial and spatiotemporal models using the *INLA package in R*
   -

   Understanding model structures for real-world applications
   -

   Working with spatial datasets using real examples and guided exercises
   -

   Applying best practices for reproducible, interpretable analysis

*Why attend?*

Small group learning, direct access to an expert tutor, and practical
sessions designed to build confidence in implementing these advanced
methods.

Whether you?re a PhD student, academic researcher, or industry analyst,
this course will provide you with the skills and understanding to apply
spatial modelling in your work immediately.

*Places are limited ? book now to secure your spot.*

Full course details and registration
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-and-spatial-temporal-modelling-using-r-inla-sstm02/>

If you have any questions, contact Oliver Hooker at:
*oliverhooker at prstatistics.com
<oliverhooker at prstatistics.com>*

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Master Mixed Models – Essential Tools for Complex Biological Data
messages.subject_clean: Master Mixed Models – Essential Tools for Complex Biological Data
messages.body_plain:
*Master Mixed Models ? Essential Tools for Complex Biological Data*

Working with repeated or hierarchical data? This course is for you.

Join our practical course: *Introduction to Mixed Models Using R and
RStudio
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-mixed-models-using-r-and-rstudio-immr09/>*

*Course dates:* 27th May 2025

*Location:* Online Live Course

Biological and ecological data often involve nested or repeated
measurements that require more flexible statistical approaches. This course
gives you the tools to analyse such data using linear mixed-effects models
in R.

Led by experienced instructors, you?ll learn:

   -

   How and when to use mixed models
   -

   The structure and interpretation of random effects
   -

   Fitting models using lme4 and other R packages
   -

   Dealing with nested, grouped, and repeated measures
   -

   Visualising and reporting mixed model results

*Who should attend?*

Researchers in biology, ecology, and related fields who already use R and
need to handle complex or hierarchical data.

*Why join us?*

Get a strong grounding in mixed model theory and its real-world
application?backed by hands-on coding, small group sessions, and biological
datasets.

*Spaces are limited ? reserve your spot today.*

? View full course details and register now
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-mixed-models-using-r-and-rstudio-immr09/>

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Learn Generalised Linear Models in R – No Prior Stats Needed
messages.subject_clean: Learn Generalised Linear Models in R – No Prior Stats Needed
messages.body_plain:
*Statistical Modelling Made Accessible ? Learn GLMs in R*

Looking to analyse biological data with greater confidence?

Join our course: *Introduction to Generalised Linear Models Using R and
RStudio
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-generalised-linear-models-using-r-and-rstudio-iglm08/>*

*Course dates:* 22?26 July 2025

*Location:* Online Live Course

This course introduces generalised linear models (GLMs) using R and is
tailored for researchers in biology, ecology, and environmental sciences.
No prior experience with statistical modelling is required.

The course covers:

   -

   The theory and structure of GLMs
   -

   Applying GLMs to biological datasets
   -

   Working with different response types (e.g. binary, count)
   -

   Interpreting model outputs in context
   -

   Good practice in coding and reporting results

*Who should attend?*

Biologists, ecologists, and environmental researchers new to GLMs or
statistical modelling in general.

*Why join us?*

Clear teaching, real-world datasets, and a supportive environment to help
you gain confidence with statistical tools in R.

*Spaces are limited ? register now to secure your place.*

? View full course details and register now
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-generalised-linear-models-using-r-and-rstudio-iglm08/>


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Learn Data Visualization in R – From Data Wrangling to Interactive Plots (30 June – 2 July)
messages.subject_clean: Learn Data Visualization in R – From Data Wrangling to Interactive Plots (30 June – 2 July)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course: "Data Visualization in R: From Data Wrangling to Interactive Plots - 30 June ? 2 July 2025 | 1?5 PM (Berlin time)
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course56/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course56/ ) 
 
In this 3-day hands-on course, you'll learn how to transform raw data into clear, compelling visuals using R. Starting with data wrangling and progressing to interactive plots, the course covers essential tools like ggplot2, plotly, shiny, and the tidyverse.
 
Whether you're new to R or want to sharpen your visualization skills, this course will equip you with practical scripts, templates, and the confidence to tackle real-world datasets.
 
 
If you're new to R, we also recommend our Intro to R with the Tidyverse course as a great foundation:[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-tidyverse/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-tidyverse/ ) 
We hope to see you online!
Best regards,

Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course: Mapping Trait Evolution in R, May 2025, online
messages.subject_clean: Course: Mapping Trait Evolution in R, May 2025, online
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

There are a few places available for the online course MAPPING TRAIT 
EVOLUTION.

Dates and schedule: Online live sessions on May 26th, 28th, and 30th & 
June 2nd and 4th, 2025, from 14:00 to 18:00 (Madrid time zone).

Instructor: Jeroen B. Smaers (Stony Brook University, USA).

Course webpage: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/evolution/mapping-trait-evolution/ 
or writing courses at transmittingscience.com

COURSE OVERVIEW

The course provides a comprehensive overview of the state-of-the-art 
methods in mapping phenotypic trait evolution and will provide 
participants with a springboard to using these methods to answering 
their own research questions.

We focus on analyses that use a phylogenetic tree and observed trait 
information from tip taxa (extant and/or extinct) to describe how traits 
have changed along the branches of a phylogeny. The course covers 
methods that estimate and test patterns related to changes in mean, 
covariation, and rate. Applications for continuous and categorical, and 
univariate and multivariate research designs are discussed.

At the end of this course, participants will have developed an 
understanding of:
(1) Brownian motion and Ornstein-Uhlenbeck models of evolution.
(2) How these models can be applied to estimate and test patterns of 
trait evolution.
(3) What the advantages and disadvantages are of different 
models/methods.
(4) How to recognize which model/method is most appropriate given a 
particular dataset and research question.

We provide several data sets that will be used to exemplify the 
application of these methods. We do, however, encourage participants to 
work with their own data so as to get direct experience with analysing 
precisely what they expect to analyse.
Methods from the following R packages will be discussed: ape, geiger, 
phytools, evomap, l1ou, bayou, surface, OUwie, mvMORPH, geomorph (this 
list may change as new packages become available).

Important note: Please bear in mind that this course is not about 
reconstructing (building) phylogenetic trees, the methods we cover in 
this course assume that a phylogeny is known.

If you have any doubts or questions, do not hesitate to write to 
courses at transmittingscience.com

Best wishes

Sole

-- 
Soledad De Esteban-Trivigno, PhD
Director
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com/courses

Bluesky @soledeesteban.bsky.social
X @SoleDeEsteban

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Movement Ecology Using R - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Movement Ecology Using R - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 5-day seminar, Movement Ecology Using R <https://instats.org/seminar/movement-ecology-using-r>, running livestreaming May 12th?16th, led by professors Luca B?rger from the Department of Biosciences, Swansea University and Garrett Street from the Forest and Wildlife Research Center, Mississippi State University. Understanding how organisms?from microorganisms to migratory megafauna?navigate their environments is central to modern ecology and evolution, and this intensive workshop equips you with the cutting-edge tools needed to analyze such movement data in R and RStudio. Across five days of expert instruction, you?ll delve into core principles of Movement Ecology, learn to work with ringing/CMR, VHF, and GPS datasets, and master statistical and mathematical modeling techniques that unite ecology, physics, and mathematics within a single analytical framework. Through hands-on coding sessions, you?ll gain practical experience in data wrangling, visualization, and advanced modeling, while also developing strategies for tackling common challenges, selecting appropriate tracking technologies, and translating results into actionable insights for conservation and management. By the end of the workshop, you will be prepared to take your movement analyses from raw data to publication-ready outputs and communicate your findings effectively to diverse audiences. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/movement-ecology-using-r> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course - Species Distribution Modeling with Bayesian Methods (22–24 September)
messages.subject_clean: Online course - Species Distribution Modeling with Bayesian Methods (22–24 September)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course: Species Distribution Modeling with Bayesian Additive Regression Tree (BART) Methods.
 
Dates: 22?24 September

Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/barts/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/barts/ ) 
 
This course will introduce participants to BART, a powerful and flexible machine learning method increasingly used in species distribution modeling (SDM). You?ll learn how BART improves over traditional SDM methods and gain hands-on experience using the embarcadero and dbarts R packages to:

Select informative environmental predictors

Train and evaluate BART models

Predict species presence or absence from new data

Interpret and visualize model outputs
This course is ideal for advanced students, researchers, and professionals with prior experience in SDM and working knowledge of R and RStudio.
 
 
If you do not have prior experience with SDM, we have an introductory course on SDM the week before: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course45/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course45/ ) 
 
 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course: Macroecology in R: Exploring Biodiversity Patterns at Large Scales, online, June 2025
messages.subject_clean: Course: Macroecology in R: Exploring Biodiversity Patterns at Large Scales, online, June 2025
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

Transmitting Science is offering a new course that may be of interest: 
"Macroecology in R: Exploring Biodiversity Patterns at Large Scales."

Places are limited to 16 participants.

Dates and schedule: Online live sessions from June 23rd to 27th, 2025, 
from 10:00-14:30 (Madrid time zone).

Course webpage: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/ecology/macroecology-in-r-exploring-biodiversity-patterns-at-large-scales/

COURSE OVERVIEW

This course provides a primer on Macroecology, focusing on geographic 
patterns of biodiversity and how those patterns are affected by species 
evolution and adaptation over time. It combines historical components of 
geographic patterns, phylogenies, and ecology to provide a comprehensive 
overview of evolutionary macroecology.

The course has a strong practical component in R. Students will learn to 
manipulate large geographic databases in R, grid-map biodiversity in 
various forms, conduct analyses of biogeographic regionalization 
incorporating evolutionary history, and estimate phylogenetic turnover 
and historical dispersal in geographic space. The practical sessions 
also cover common metrics in community phylogenetics and 
diversification, focusing on large geographic scales.

At the end of this course, participants will be able to:

  	* Generate geographic maps of biodiversity (species diversity, trait 
diversity, etc.).
  	* Use phylogenies to incorporate evolution into geographic patterns of 
diversity.
  	* Construct biogeographic regions while accounting for the 
evolutionary relationship of species.
  	* Map historical patterns of diversification, historical dispersal, 
and phylogenetic turnover.
  	* Calculate and map in-situ diversification, the age of assemblages, 
and new tip-based metrics of trait evolution.
  	* Associate geographic patterns of biodiversity with ecological 
predictors.
  	* Integrate tools of macroevolutionary dynamics, such as estimating 
ancestral areas and ancestral traits, with community phylogenetic 
metrics.

Several datasets will be provided to illustrate the application of these 
methods. Participants are encouraged to bring their own datasets to 
analyze and discuss with the instructor.

If you have any doubts or questions, do not hesitate to write to 
courses at transmittingscience.com

Best wishes

Sole

-- 
Soledad De Esteban-Trivigno, PhD
Director
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com/courses

Bluesky @soledeesteban.bsky.social
X @SoleDeEsteban
Orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2049-0890

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Model Species Distributions with Bayesian Statistics in R | Online Course | 30 Sep - 2 Oc t
messages.subject_clean: Model Species Distributions with Bayesian Statistics in R | Online Course | 30 Sep - 2 Oc t
messages.body_plain:
*Master Bayesian Species Distribution Modelling ? Tailored for Ecologists*
ONLINE COURSE ? Species Distribution Modelling With Bayesian Statistics
Using R (SDMB07) This course will be delivered live
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-using-r-sdmb07/>

Ready to take your species distribution modelling to the next level?

Join our advanced online course:

*Species Distribution Modelling with Bayesian Statistics Using R*

*Course dates:* 30th Sept to 2nd Oct 2025

*Location:* Live and Online

*Instructor: *Dr Marcia Barbosa

This course introduces ecologists and environmental scientists to Bayesian
approaches for species distribution modelling (SDM) using R. You?ll learn
how to build, fit, and interpret SDMs using modern Bayesian tools and
ecological datasets.

Led by expert instructors, the course covers:

   -

   Bayesian model structure and thinking
   -

   Implementing SDMs in a Bayesian framework
   -

   Model fitting using R
   -

   Interpreting outputs and mapping predictions

*Who should attend?*

Ecologists, spatial analysts, and researchers with some experience in R and
basic SDMs who want to build models using a Bayesian approach.

*Why join us?*

Gain cutting-edge tools for ecological modelling, strengthen your
statistical understanding, and produce models that reflect the uncertainty
and complexity of real-world systems.

*Places are limited ? book early to secure your space.*

View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-using-r-sdmb07/>

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject_clean: Transform How You Work with Data – Learn the Tidyverse way
messages.body_plain:
*Transform How You Work with Data ? Learn the Tidyverse way*
ONLINE COURSE ? Tidyverse for Ecologists (TIDY01) This course will be
delivered live
<https://www.prstats.org/course/tidyverse-for-ecologists-tidy01/>

Want to clean, reshape, and visualise ecological data more efficiently?

Join our hands-on course: *Tidyverse for Ecologists*

*Course dates:* 16-20th June 2025

*Location:* Live and Online

*Instructor:* Dr Gabriel Palma

This practical course introduces the Tidyverse?a suite of powerful,
user-friendly R packages for data manipulation and visualisation?tailored
specifically to the needs of ecologists and life science researchers.

Led by experienced instructors, you?ll learn to:

   -

   Import, clean, and reshape complex datasets
   -

   Use dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, and other core Tidyverse tools
   -

   Streamline your data analysis workflow
   -

   Produce clear, publication-quality plots
   -

   Work with real-world ecological data

*Who should attend?*

Ecologists, biologists, and environmental researchers who are familiar with
basic R and want to work more effectively with data.

*Why join us?*

Learn reproducible, modern R workflows in a small-group setting with
applied ecological examples and expert guidance.

*Places are limited ? secure your spot now.*

View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/tidyverse-for-ecologists-tidy01/>

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Data Visualization with ggplot2 - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Data Visualization with ggplot2 - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Data Visualization with ggplot2 <https://instats.org/seminar/data-visualization-with-ggplot2>, livestreaming on May 19 and led by the Statistical Methodology and Consultancy Service (SMCS) of UC Louvain. Effective visuals are indispensable for exploring data, uncovering patterns, and communicating results, and ggplot2?the gold-standard R package?lets you turn complex datasets into clear, publication-ready graphics. Tailored to PhD candidates, faculty, and applied researchers, this intensive workshop moves from visualization fundamentals through advanced customization, layering, annotation, and best practices, equipping you to transform raw numbers into persuasive narratives and to streamline your exploratory analysis workflow across disciplines. A basic familiarity with R is recommended, and those needing a refresher can join Instats? free Introduction to R seminar to get up to speed beforehand. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/data-visualization-with-ggplot2> to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject_clean: Analyse Ecological Communities with Confidence – Learn VEGAN in R
messages.body_plain:
*Multivariate Tools for Ecologists ? Get to Grips with the Vegan Package*

Want to analyse species composition, community structure, and ecological
gradients?

Join our in-depth course: *Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities
Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR08)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr08/>*

*Course dates:* 15-19th September 2025

*Location:* Live and Online
Instructor: Dr. Antoine Becker-Scarpitta
This course is designed for ecologists and environmental scientists who
want to apply multivariate techniques using R?s*vegan* package. You?ll gain
hands-on experience with the tools used to interpret species-environment
relationships and community composition.

Taught by expert instructors, you?ll learn to:

   -

   Understand the principles of multivariate analysis
   -

   Apply ordination methods (PCA, NMDS, CCA, RDA)
   -

   Work with dissimilarity matrices and ecological distance measures
   -

   Analyse species?environment relationships
   -

   Use vegan effectively for real-world datasets

*Who should attend?*

Field ecologists, biodiversity researchers, and community ecologists who
already have some R experience and want to apply robust multivariate
methods to ecological data.

*Why join us?*

Deepen your statistical toolkit, sharpen your interpretation of community
data, and gain confidence in your analysis using vegan.

*Spaces are limited ? reserve your place now.*

 View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr08/>

Email oliverhooker at prstatitics.com with any questions

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Introduction to Experimental Design - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to Experimental Design - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is thrilled to offer a 1-day seminar, Introduction to Experimental Design: Concepts and Applications <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-experimental-design-conc>, livestreaming on May 22 and led by Dr Byran Smucker from the Department of Public Health Sciences, Division of Biostatistics, Henry Ford Health. Experimental design underpins credible, reproducible research across psychology, medicine, engineering, and beyond; this intensive workshop equips academics, graduate students, and industry practitioners with the practical philosophy of experimentation?highlighting randomization, replication, and blocking?while walking through power analysis, one-factor studies, and more advanced designs. Through real-world examples and R-based demonstrations (no prior R experience required), participants will learn to distinguish exploratory from confirmatory studies, avoid common pitfalls, and implement simple yet powerful confirmatory experiments that stand up to rigorous scrutiny.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-experimental-design-conc> to secure your spot, and please share with colleagues and students who may benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course: Generalised Linear Mixed-Effects Models
messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Generalised Linear Mixed-Effects Models
messages.body_plain:
*Online Course*: Generalised Linear Mixed-Effects Models (GLMMs)
*Dates*: 19?23 May 2025
*Format*: Live online via Zoom
*Times*: 08:30?15:30 (Norwegian time)
*Fee*: ?500 (50% discount for participants from developing countries)
*Flyer*: https://www.highstat.com/Courses/Flyers/Flyer2025_05_IMR_GLMM.pdf

Join us for a practical, hands-on introduction to GLMMs?ideal for 
analysing ecological and fisheries data with hierarchical or clustered 
structure. We begin with linear regression and data exploration, then 
move into mixed-effects models and GLMMs for count, binary, 
proportional, and continuous data (with or without zeros). Topics 
include model diagnostics, overdispersion, zero-inflation, and 
visualisation using a wide range of distributions (Poisson, negative 
binomial, binomial, beta, gamma, Tweedie, and more). Around 15 
real-world exercises (with on-demand video) are included.


*Bonus*: The course includes a 1-hour personal consultation with the 
instructors to discuss your own data.

*Provided by*: Highland Statistics Ltd.

Kind regards,

Alain Zuur

www.highstat.com

messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Introduction to Statistics in R (23–26 June)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
there are a few seats left for the Physalia online course "Introduction to Statistics in R", taking place from 23?26 June.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ) 
 
This hands-on 4-day course is ideal for early-career researchers and practitioners with no prior experience in statistics or programming. Participants will learn how to use R and the tidyverse for data exploration, descriptive and inferential statistics, linear models, and more.
 
The course includes:

Introduction to tidyverse, data cleaning, and ggplot2 for visualization

Core concepts in descriptive and inferential statistics

Practical use of linear, generalised, and mixed models

Step-by-step R scripting for reproducible and readable analyses
 
Should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Carlo

messages.subject_clean: Advanced Methods in Social Network Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 2-day seminar, Advanced Methods in Social Network Analysis <https://instats.org/seminar/advanced-methods-in-social-network-analy>, running Livestreaming May 22?24 and led by professor Antonio Zinilli from the Research Institute on Sustainable Economic Growth at the National Research Council of Italy (CNR). As networks increasingly underpin social, economic, political, and health phenomena, mastering their analysis is essential for contemporary research. Over two intensive days, Professor Zinilli will take participants from foundational SNA concepts to advanced techniques?including Exponential Random Graph Models?through an engaging blend of theory and hands-on practice in R. You will learn how to structure and clean network data, calculate and interpret centrality measures, perform sophisticated modelling and simulations, and craft compelling visualisations and reports that translate complex relational information into publishable insights.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/advanced-methods-in-social-network-analy> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

ineq

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] ONLINE COURSE – Introduction To Mixed Models Using R And Rstudio (IMMR09)
messages.subject_clean: ONLINE COURSE – Introduction To Mixed Models Using R And Rstudio (IMMR09)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Introduction To Mixed Models Using R And Rstudio (IMMR09)
https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-mixed-models-using-r-and-rstudio-immr09/

27th - 29th May 2025

This course provides a comprehensive practical and theoretical
introduction to multilevel models, also known as hierarchical or mixed
effects models. We will focus primarily on multilevel linear models,
but also cover multilevel generalized linear models. Likewise, we will
also describe Bayesian approaches to multilevel modelling. We will
begin by focusing on random effects multilevel models. These models
make it clear how multilevel models are in fact models of models. In
addition, random effects models serve as a solid basis for
understanding mixed effects, i.e. fixed and random effects, models. In
this coverage of random effects, we will also cover the important
concepts of statistical shrinkage in the estimation of effects, as
well as intraclass correlation. We then proceed to cover linear mixed
effects models, particularly focusing on varying intercept and/or
varying slopes regression models. We will then cover further aspects
of linear mixed effects models, including multilevel models for nested
and crossed data data, and group level predictor variables. Towards
the end of the course we also cover generalized linear mixed models
(GLMMs), how to accommodate overdispersion through individual-level
random effects, as well as Bayesian approaches to multilevel levels
using the brms R package.

*Day 1*

Topic 1: Random effects models. The defining feature of multilevel models
is that they are models of models. We begin by using a binomial random
effects model to illustrate this. Specifically, we show how multilevel
models are models of the variability in models of different clusters or
groups of data.

Topic 2: Normal random effects models. Normal, as in normal distribution,
random effects models are the key to understanding the more general and
widely used linear mixed effects models. Here, we also cover the key
concepts of statistical shrinkage and intraclass correlation.

*Day 2*

Topic 3: Linear mixed effects models. Next, we turn to multilevel linear
models, also known as linear mixed effects models. We specifically deal
with the cases of varying intercept and/or varying slope linear regression
models.

Topic 4: Multilevel models for nested data. Here, we will consider
multilevel linear models for nested, as in groups of groups, data. As an
example, we will look at multilevel linear models applied to data from
students within classes that are themselves within different schools, and
where we model the variability of effects across the classes and across the
schools.

Topic 5: Multilevel models for crossed data. In some multilevel models,
each observation occurs in multiple groups, but these groups are not
nested. For example, animals may be members of different species and in
different locations, but the species are not subsets of locations, nor vice
versa. These are known as crossed or multiclass data structures.

*Day 3*

Topic 6: Group level predictors. In some multilevel regression models,
predictor variable are sometimes associated with individuals, and sometimes
associated with their groups. In this section, we consider how to handle
these two situations.

Topic 7: Generalized linear mixed models (GLMMs). Here, we extend the
linear mixed model to the exponential family of distributions and showcase
an example using the Poisson GLMM. We also cover how to accommodate
overdispersion through individual-level random effects.

Topic 8: Bayesian multilevel models. All of the models that we have
considered can be handled, often more easily, using Bayesian models. Here,
we provide an brief introduction to Bayesian models and how to perform
examples of the models that we have considered using Bayesian methods and
the brms R package.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions.

Please feel free to share among colleagues and friends


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
ONLINE COURSE ? Introduction To Mixed Models Using R And Rstudio (IMMR09)
https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-mixed-models-using-r-and-rstudio-immr09/

27th - 29th May 2025

This course pro

Dear all,

This hands-on tutorial will introduce participants to the fundamentals of data.table through live coding sessions and practical exercises. Participants will learn how to integrate data.table into a typical data analysis workflow?from data import and exploration to transformation and export. The course also serves as an introduction to more advanced data.table features, such as special symbols and combined operations.

Efficiently read and write data using fread() and fwrite()

Understand and apply the core syntax of data.table

Perform fast row subsetting and column operations

Use data.table for data exploration, transformation, and summarization

Begin exploring more advanced functionalities such as special symbols and combined operations

Dear all,

I'm trying to do an analysis that is a bit unusual and I'd appreciate any
feedback. I'm looking at correlates of body size, but I want to analyze
males and females separately. However, a regular PGLS would only allow one
value per tip. I'd like something like this:

body size ~ correlate1 + correlate2 + sex

Any suggestions?

Karla

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course – Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R (9–13 June)
messages.subject_clean: Course – Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R (9–13 June)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

We?re excited to announce that there are only a few seats left for our upcoming online course:Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R
Dates: 9?13 June 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biodiversity-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biodiversity-in-r/ )
 
This hands-on course will guide you through building reproducible workflows for acquiring, cleaning, and analysing biodiversity data from both modern and fossil records. You?ll explore how diversity patterns vary across space and time, and learn to address common sampling challenges using powerful R tools and packages.
 
By the end of the course, you will be able to:

Access and clean biodiversity occurrence data from major databases (GBIF, PBDB, IUCN)

Analyse biodiversity patterns across spatial and temporal scales

Visualise biodiversity data and estimate diversification rates using R

Integrate palaeontological and neontological datasets for comprehensive biodiversity analysis
 
Best regards, Carlo
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Introduction to Statistics with R
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to Statistics with R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
there are only a few seats left for the upcoming Physalia online course, "Introduction to Statistics in R," scheduled for 23?26 June.
This course is designed for early-career researchers, PhD students, and professionals seeking to enhance their data analysis capabilities using R. No prior experience in statistics or programming is required.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ) 
 
In this course, attendees will get:

Comprehensive introduction to R and the tidyverse

Exploration of descriptive and inferential statistical methods

Hands-on sessions covering linear and generalized linear models

Guidance on reproducible and efficient data analysis practices
Should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Efficient Data Wrangling with R data.table - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Efficient Data Wrangling with R data.table - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Efficient Data Wrangling with R data.table <https://instats.org/seminar/efficient-data-wrangling-with-r-datatabl>, livestreaming on June 4 and led by Dr Fodil Ihaddaden from the Institute for Statistical and Data Science. Designed for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers who grapple with large datasets, this workshop showcases how the data.table package can radically improve speed and efficiency over base R and dplyr. Participants will explore core syntax, keys and indexing, rapid filtering and mutating, advanced aggregation, memory-conscious handling of big data, grouped operations, and strategies for integrating data.table with other R tools, all through hands-on exercises that translate directly to real research challenges.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/efficient-data-wrangling-with-r-datatabl> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] R for Beginners: A Complete Course - livestream seminars
messages.subject_clean: R for Beginners: A Complete Course - livestream seminars
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Unlock the power of statistis and data science in R with the 5-part seminar series R for Beginners: A Complete Course <https://instats.org/structuredcourse/r-for-beginners-a-complete-course2>, starting June 11th and being offered by the SMCS group from UC Louvain?five expertly guided seminars that take you from R?s fundamentals through data manipulation, visualization, exploratory analysis, hypothesis testing, and advanced modeling (ANOVA, regression, logistic regression) using real-world research examples. Whether you join a single seminar to sharpen a specific skill or enroll in all five to build a comprehensive toolkit, you?ll gain hands-on practice, personalized feedback, and the confidence to tackle complex datasets in your PhD work, academic projects, or professional research. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/structuredcourse/r-for-beginners-a-complete-course2> for any individual session at your convenience?or commit to the full series and enjoy a 10% discount on the complete package. Reserve your spot today and elevate your research with R!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Deep Learning with ChatGPT - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Deep Learning with ChatGPT - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with ChatGPT <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with>, running June 6th and led by professor Nikolay Oskolkov from at Lund University. This intensive workshop equips PhD students, academics, and professional researchers with the practical skills needed to harness cutting-edge deep learning approaches for complex, real-world data. Through hands-on exercises in R, Python, TensorFlow, and Keras?guided by ChatGPT as an interactive coding consultant?you will learn foundational principles of machine and deep learning, build neural networks from scratch, explore convolutional and recurrent architectures for sequential and genomic data, apply autoencoders for dimensionality reduction, and examine case studies spanning multi-omics integration, single-cell biology, and personalized medicine. By the end of the day, participants will be able to design, train, evaluate, and refine their own models, integrate Large Language Models into existing analytical workflows, and take away actionable insights that immediately translate to their research.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Spatial Data Visualisation in R course
messages.subject_clean: Spatial Data Visualisation in R course
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course: Spatial Data Visualisation in RDates: 17?19 June - Online (3?6 PM Berlin time daily)Website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/spatial-dataviz/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/spatial-dataviz/ )
 
This hands-on course is perfect for anyone working with spatial or ecological data. You'll learn how to:

Visualize remote sensing data using R (including ggplot2 and raster matrices)

Analyze spatial and temporal variability

Create accessible, colorblind-friendly scientific visualizations

Simulate species distributions and apply advanced spatial techniques
No prior experience with R is required?just curiosity and an interest in spatial analysis!
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D
